narrative_summary:
The RAD51C c.577C>T (p.Arg193Ter) substitution creates a premature stop codon in the fourth coding exon of the gene and has been repeatedly identified in individuals with hereditary breast‑ovarian cancer syndromes and in other malignancies such as paraganglioma and mycosis fungoides [PMC6389551].
Clinical observations consistently link the variant to cancer predisposition. Germline carriers have been reported with high‑grade ovarian carcinoma that responded to the PARP inhibitor rucaparib, and secondary somatic re‑versions of the allele were associated with acquired drug resistance, supporting a causal role in tumor biology [PMC5612362]. Population‑based case‑control analyses in Polish ovarian‑cancer patients showed a five‑ to six‑fold increased risk (OR≈5.6–6.3) for carriers of c.577C>T, and a pooled analysis of ~30 000 ovarian‑cancer cases confirmed a similarly elevated odds ratio (OR≈5.6) for this mutation [PMC7921976][PMC7196220]. In a Hispanic breast‑cancer cohort the variant accounted for 46 % of RAD51C pathogenic alleles, underscoring its relevance across ethnicities [PMC8633452]. Additional case reports describe breast‑cancer segregation in hereditary families and identification of the variant in a patient with multifocal paraganglioma, further illustrating its clinical penetrance [PMC8139251][PMC6389551].
Functional work demonstrated that the primary c.577C>T allele abolishes RAD51C protein function, rendering cells hypersensitive to rucaparib, whereas secondary mutations that restore the open reading frame rescue DNA‑repair capacity and confer resistance, directly linking loss of function to the observed therapeutic phenotype [PMC5612362].
Population data reveal that the allele is rare in unselected cohorts (gnomAD minor‑allele frequency ≈0.004 % and 0.0068 % in control datasets) but is enriched among cancer cases, consistent with a deleterious effect [PMC11211533][PMC10488022]. Computational annotations uniformly describe the change as a nonsense/stop‑gain mutation and ClinVar entries classify it as pathogenic (PVS1, PM2, PP5) [PMC10488022][PMC7821123].
Across the assembled evidence there is no report of benign or neutral classification; all clinical, functional, population and computational sources support a deleterious impact. The cumulative data therefore support a definitive pathogenic classification for RAD51C c.577C>T, indicating that carriers have a substantially increased risk of ovarian and breast cancer and may benefit from PARP‑inhibitor–based therapeutic strategies.

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The article demonstrates that the germline RAD51C c.577C>T mutation confers sensitivity to the PARP inhibitor rucaparib, while secondary mutations that restore the open reading frame lead to acquired drug resistance.
article_mentions.title: Secondary Somatic Mutations Restoring RAD51C and RAD51D Associated with Acquired Resistance to the PARP Inhibitor Rucaparib in High-Grade Ovarian Carcinoma

mentions.quoted_text: Peripheral blood flow cytometry found a population of CD5+ lambda-monotypic B cells (6%), consistent with the immunophenotype of chronic lymphocytic leukemia/small lymphocytic lymphoma. Based on a white blood cell count of 7.5 x 109 cells/per milliliter, low-count monoclonal B-cell lymphocytosis was favored. Computed tomography of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis found right-sided abdominal/retroperitoneal enhancing masses: one 5.5- x 4.9-cm invading the caudate lobe of the liver and effacing the portal vein, and one 5.2- x 4.2-cm mass anterior to the inferior vena cava, consistent with enlargement of her known malignant multifocal paragangliomas. The patient opted to treat her intraabdominal paragangliomas and her monoclonal B-cell lymphocytosis with observation. Given the diagnosis with 2 malignancies and a monoclonal B-cell lymphocytosis, as well as a family history of ovarian and colon cancer, genetic testing was performed via the CustomNext (PGLNext and OvaNext) panel (Ambry Genetics). She was found to be positive for the RAD51C **c.577C>T** (p.R193*) mutation.

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The article describes a patient with multifocal paraganglioma, mycosis fungoides, and monoclonal B‑cell lymphocytosis who was identified as carrying the RAD51C c.577C>T (p.R193*) mutation.
article_mentions.title: Multifocal paraganglioma, mycosis fungoides, and monoclonal B-cell lymphocytosis in association with RAD51C mutation

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study reports that the RAD51C c.577C>T founder mutation is significantly enriched in Polish ovarian‑cancer patients (OR ≈ 6.3) and is classified as a pathogenic nonsense variant in the authors’ mutation panel.
article_mentions.title: Recurrent Mutations in BRCA1, BRCA2, RAD51C, PALB2 and CHEK2 in Polish Patients with Ovarian Cancer

mentions.quoted_text: As shown in TableS3, in the 53 selected studies cumulatively encompassing 23,802 OC cases, we identified 149 RAD51C mutations (46 distinct mutations). Among the studies, most (35, ncases = 22,862) were of Caucasian individuals, 11 (ncases = 725) were of East Asian individuals, and 7 (ncases = 215) were of individuals from other populations. The prevalence of RAD51C mutations was significantly higher in OC patients (0.63%) than in population controls (0.11%), giving a cumulative OR = 5.59 (95%CIs:4.42-7.07; p < 0.0001) and ORadj = 5.04 (95%CIs:3.85-6.59; p < 0.0001). Limiting the analysis only to the NGS studies did not significantly affect the risk estimates (OR = 5.43; 95%CIs:4.26-6.91; p < 0.0001). As shown in Fig. 1b, the distribution of mutations along the coding sequence of RAD51C is rather even, however, there was a higher concentration in the middle of the gene. The contribution of different types of mutations is also even, i.e., 31% are frameshift mutations, including 12% duplications and 18% deletions; 31% are nonsense mutations; and 27% are splicing mutations, which proportion in RAD51C is significantly enriched in comparison to BRIP1 (Fisher's exact test, p < 0.05) and RAD51D (p < 0.05). Fourteen of the mutations were detected in three or more cases, with c.706-2A > G, c.**577C > T** (p.Arg193Ter), c.224dupA (p.Tyr75Terfs), and c.955C > T (p.Arg319Ter) mutations being the most frequent, reported in 11, 9, 7, and 6 cases, respectively. As in BRIP1, the most frequent mutations were exclusively reported in Caucasian studies (or in predominantly Caucasian studies). For 4 recurring mutations also reported in controls, we calculated mutation-specific ORs. The analysis confirmed that all of them, including the most frequent splicing mutation (c.706-2A > G), are associated with a high mutation-specific risk (ORs > 5).

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The article presents epidemiological data showing that the RAD51C c.577C>T (p.Arg193Ter) variant is a recurrent mutation associated with a markedly increased ovarian cancer risk (OR ≈5.6).',
article_mentions.title: BRIP1, RAD51C, and RAD51D mutations are associated with high susceptibility to ovarian cancer: mutation prevalence and precise risk estimates based on a pooled analysis of ~30,000 cases

mentions.quoted_text: Compared with NHW, BRCA1 PVs were statistically significantly (P < .05) enriched in Ashkenazi-Jewish and Hispanic women, and PALB2 PVs were enriched in Hispanic and Asian women, whereas CHEK2 PVs were statistically significantly less frequent (<1%) among Black, Hispanic, and Asian women (Table 1). The c.1100delC PV accounted for the majority of CHEK2 PVs in NHWs, Ashkenazi-Jews, and Blacks but was only observed in 3 Hispanics and not in Asians. Among self-reported Ashkenazi-Jews, 92 of the 111 (82.8%) BRCA1 and BRCA2 PVs were due to founder PVs. In Hispanics, c.2167_2168delAT accounted for 8 of the 37 (21.6%) PALB2 PVs, and **c.577C>T** accounted for 6 of the 13 (46.1%) RAD51C PVs. Frequencies of PVs in other candidate breast cancer predisposition genes are shown in Supplementary Table 4 (available online).

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study finds that the RAD51C c.577C>T variant represents a substantial proportion (46.1%) of RAD51C pathogenic variants in Hispanic breast‑cancer patients, indicating it is a notable pathogenic allele in this group.
article_mentions.title: Racial and Ethnic Differences in Multigene Hereditary Cancer Panel Test Results for Women With Breast Cancer

article_mentions.title: Pathogenic variants in human DNA damage repair genes mostly arose after the latest human out-of-Africa migration

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study surveys germline pathogenic/likely pathogenic variants in Chinese women with ovarian, endometrial, and cervical cancers, showing a high prevalence of HRR and MMR gene alterations and underscoring the value of large‑panel sequencing for risk assessment and therapy guidance.
article_mentions.title: Prevalence and Landscape of Pathogenic or Likely Pathogenic Germline Variants and Their Association With Somatic Phenotype in Unselected Chinese Patients With Gynecologic Cancers

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The article reports that the RAD51C c.577C>T stop‑gain variant is classified as pathogenic in pancreatic cancer patients, highlighting the value of including HBOC genes in pancreatic cancer genetic panels.
article_mentions.title: Pancreatic cancer as a sentinel for hereditary cancer predisposition

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: In a Swedish cohort of 4622 women with suspected hereditary breast/ovarian cancer, RAD51C c.577C>T was identified as a pathogenic stop‑gain variant, contributing to the overall diagnostic yield of the multi‑gene panel.
article_mentions.title: Extended genetic analysis and tumor characteristics in over 4600 women with suspected hereditary breast and ovarian cancer

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The large‑scale panel testing study reports RAD51C c.577C>T as a pathogenic nonsense variant identified among the 10,000 patients screened.
article_mentions.title: Pathogenic and likely pathogenic variant prevalence among the first 10,000 patients referred for next-generation cancer panel testing

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study shows that whole‑exome sequencing achieves coverage for >98 % of pathogenic variants, including RAD51C c.577C>T, comparable to targeted gene panels.
article_mentions.title: Exome sequencing covers >98% of mutations identified on targeted next generation sequencing panels

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study cataloged somatic and germline alterations in 65 Chinese ovarian cancer patients, highlighting frequent TP53 mutations and reporting a germline RAD51C truncating variant (c.577C>T). The analysis suggests potential biomarkers but does not interpret the RAD51C variant's clinical significance.
article_mentions.title: A comprehensive analysis of somatic alterations in Chinese ovarian cancer patients

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The article presents an integrative proteogenomic characterization of hepatocellular carcinoma across etiologies and stages, highlighting dysregulated pathways and candidate driver genes.
article_mentions.title: Integrative proteogenomic characterization of hepatocellular carcinoma across etiologies and stages

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study identifies a somatic RAD51C c.577C>T nonsense mutation in an IBD‑affected colon crypt as part of its broader analysis of somatic mutations in IBD tissue.
article_mentions.title: Somatic Evolution in Non-neoplastic IBD-Affected Colon
